summary: Small item-code matching PR driven by a new permuter, touching Link boomerang, Link hookshot, and Ness yoyo. The changes are mostly local source-shape adjustments: temporary aliases, inline helper extraction, declaration/order reshuffling, stack padding changes, boolean cleanup, and one suspicious-looking hookshot argument correction from fp->x40 to attr->x40. The PR body says the new permuter yielded a few wins and reports remaining item work as 21 item TUs and 69 item functions.
what_changed: In itlinkboomerang.c, it_802A0534 now initializes gobj->hsd_obj through an intermediate HSD_JObj* jobj_tmp before assigning jobj, a source-shape tweak likely found by the permuter.

In itlinkhookshot.c, fn_802A33A0 gained a static inline helper fn_802A33A0_GetFighter(Item*) returning item->owner->user_data; local declarations were reordered around Item, Fighter, attributes, and ItemLink.
In fn_802A33A0, the call to it_802A5E28 changed its final argument from fp->x40 to attr->x40.

In it_802A39FC, Item* item and itLinkHookshotAttributes* attr are initialized at declaration time, the temporary collision/check variable changed from int var_r0 to bool chk, PAD_STACK(4) was removed, and a small scoped y_delta temporary was inserted before assigning temp_f30.
In it_802A4758, a static inline helper it_802A4758_permuterslop(ItemLink*) was added to return &link_0->pos, and test_comp now receives that helper result instead of &link_0->pos directly.

In itnessyoyo.c, the rotation update portion of itNessyoyo_UnkMotion3_Anim was extracted into static inline itNessyoyo_UnkMotion3_Anim_UpdateRotation(Item*), with PAD_STACK adjusted from 24 in the old function body to 16 in the helper plus 16 in the caller.
The PR reduced remaining item work according to the body: 21 remaining item translation units and 69 remaining item functions.
smart_moves: Used very small static inline wrappers to affect compiler codegen without changing runtime behavior, e.g. fn_802A33A0_GetFighter and it_802A4758_permuterslop.

Preserved evidence of intentionally awkward matching-only code with comments such as '// permuterslop' and the commented-out candidate helper call '// it_802A39FC_InitState(gobj, &item, &attr, &fp);'.
Changed an integer flag used as a boolean into bool chk, matching the apparent true/false control-flow shape while keeping behavior equivalent.
Extracted Ness yoyo JObj rotation update into a separate inline, giving the compiler a different stack/local layout while keeping the public animation function behavior intact.
Inserted narrow temporary scopes and aliases, such as y_delta and jobj_tmp, which are common decomp matching tactics for influencing register allocation and stack layout.
decomp_lessons: Permuter wins may involve intentionally non-idiomatic source shapes: temporary aliases, single-purpose inline accessors, scoped locals, and declaration reordering can matter for matching.
When matching item functions, keep owner/item/fighter access patterns flexible; wrapping item->owner->user_data in a static inline can improve codegen while preserving semantics.
Stack padding may need to move when code is extracted into an inline helper; in this PR, Ness yoyo rotation logic changed from one PAD_STACK(24) site to PAD_STACK(16) in both helper/caller contexts.
A direct address expression such as &link_0->pos may not compile the same as a helper returning that address; consider helper indirection when a stubborn expression fails to match.
Declaration order and initialization timing can be matching-relevant, as shown by initializing item and attr at declaration in it_802A39FC and reordering fp/item_link locals in fn_802A33A0.
The diff includes one semantic-looking argument change from fp->x40 to attr->x40; future readers should verify this against symbols/struct fields before treating it as merely cosmetic permuter slop.
naming_conventions: Matching-only helper names in this slice are function-scoped or callsite-scoped, e.g. fn_802A33A0_GetFighter and it_802A4758_permuterslop.
The suffix '_permuterslop' is used explicitly for a helper that exists to satisfy compiler/permuter shape rather than to encode domain meaning.
Extracted helper names follow the parent function name plus a descriptive action, e.g. itNessyoyo_UnkMotion3_Anim_UpdateRotation.
Temporary names such as jobj_tmp, y_delta, chk, link_0, and link_1 reflect local codegen/matching intent rather than high-level gameplay semantics.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Introduce a redundant local alias before use: HSD_JObj* jobj_tmp = gobj->hsd_obj; HSD_JObj* jobj = jobj_tmp.
Replace a direct field access with a static inline getter to alter inlining/register behavior: fn_802A33A0_GetFighter(item).
Use a static inline that returns a field address to change how an address expression is materialized: it_802A4758_permuterslop(link_0) returning &link_0->pos.
Move local initialization into declarations to alter lifetime/order, as with Item* item = gobj->user_data and attr initialization in it_802A39FC.
Use bool true/false assignments instead of integer 1/0 when the compiler shape benefits from explicit boolean control flow.
Introduce nested scopes and one-use temporaries, such as f32 y_delta, to steer float register assignment or store timing.
Extract a block of logic into a static inline helper while adjusting PAD_STACK to preserve caller/helper stack layout.
review_feedback: No review comments or formal review feedback were present in the provided PR slice.
The only human body text says: 'New permuter yielded a few wins. :)' and reports remaining item TUs/functions.
follow_up_queries: Check whether the fn_802A33A0 it_802A5E28 argument change from fp->x40 to attr->x40 was required for correctness, matching, or both.
Look for later PRs that remove or rename helpers marked permuterslop after surrounding item TUs become better understood.
Search for other item matching PRs by lukechampine using static inline wrappers to influence address-taking or owner/fighter access.
Compare remaining item TU/function counts before and after PR #2579 to identify exactly which functions became matched.
